metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

there are criticisms about my relationship
it's weird for a 58 year old man to be with me
I don't see age being an issue
I'm waking up with those thoughts

patient.query: I guess it’s just the way they talk about relationships like it’s this checklist. If I don’t match up to what they think is “right,” I suddenly feel like I’m doing something wrong. I mean, I care about what they think, but it just makes me feel like I’m not enough sometimes.

automatic_thoughts: I’m doing something wrong
I’m not enough

I start to compare what I have with Stu to this ideal, and it just... makes me feel really anxious. I worry that maybe I'm not giving enough, or that I'm missing something important, even if things are going pretty well.

I'm not giving enough

patient.query: Well, I think they’d see that we have fun together. We laugh, we connect over silly things, and I feel comfortable being myself. But I also wonder if they’d notice how I sometimes overthink everything after we hang out, like I'm waiting for the other shoe to drop or something. It’s like, the joy is there, but so is this nagging worry, and that kind of dims the light on what we have.

automatic_thoughts: they might notice my overthinking
waiting for the other shoe to drop
the joy is overshadowed by worry

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely puts a damper on things. Like, when I'm with Stu, I’m having a great time, but then I start replaying conversations in my head, thinking, “Did I say the right thing?” or “Are we really that good together?” It’s exhausting because instead of just being in the moment, I get caught up in this loop of doubt and concern. I want to just enjoy him, but the worry feels like this weight I can’t shake off.

Are we really that good together?

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. It’s like, I know I should be enjoying what we have, but then there's this nagging voice in my head, telling me that I should be doing better or that something’s off. Sometimes I wonder if I'm just sabotaging myself for no good reason. I feel guilty for feeling this way when things are actually good, you know?

patient.query: It’s like this constant whispering, kind of judging and critical. It tells me I should be more spontaneous or that I need to prove myself somehow. And then it spirals into thoughts like, “What if he finds someone better?” or “What if I mess this up?” It's hard to silence it, especially when I really care about him. It just makes it all so heavy.

What if he finds someone better?

patient.query: Yeah, it's really tough. I mean, when I think about how much I like him and how genuine our connection feels, I want to just lean into that. But then, every time someone brings up relationships or starts comparing, it stirs up all this anxiety I didn’t even realize I was carrying. It's like, I want to be happy, but these expectations just keep creeping back in and overshadowing everything. I know it shouldn’t affect me this much, but it does.

these expectations keep creeping back in
I know it shouldn’t affect me this much
